data.content:
### Install Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/contributing/getting-started.mdx

Installs the project's dependencies using pnpm.

```sh
pnpm install
```

--------------------------------

### Verify OpenStatus CLI Installation

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/cli/getting-started.mdx

Verifies that the OpenStatus CLI has been installed correctly by displaying the help message.

```bash
openstatus --help
```

--------------------------------

### Install Devbox and Start Services

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/README.md

Instructions for installing Devbox and starting project services using Devbox commands.

```sh
curl -fsSL https://get.jetify.com/devbox | bash
devbox services up
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenStatus CLI on Windows

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/cli/getting-started.mdx

Installs the OpenStatus CLI on Windows using PowerShell. This command downloads and executes an installation script.

```powershell
iwr instl.sh/openstatushq/cli/windows | iex
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Development Environment

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/contributing/getting-started.mdx

Launches the development database using Turso.

```sh
turso dev --db-file openstatus-dev.db
```

--------------------------------

### Clone Repository

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/contributing/getting-started.mdx

Clones the OpenStatus project repository from GitHub.

```sh
git clone https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus.git
```

--------------------------------

### Run Development Command

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/contributing/getting-started.mdx

Executes the development command for the project.

```sh
pnpm dx
```

--------------------------------

### Start DB Studio

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/contributing/setup-env.mdx

Navigates to the `packages/db` directory and starts the database studio using `pnpm studio`. This command is used to interact with and manage the project's database.

```bash
cd packages/db
pnpm studio
```

--------------------------------

### Launch Web App

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/contributing/getting-started.mdx

Starts the web application, typically running on port 3000.

```sh
pnpm dev:web
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenStatus CLI on macOS/Linux via Script

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/cli/getting-started.mdx

Installs the OpenStatus CLI on macOS or Linux by downloading and executing a script. This is an alternative to using Homebrew on macOS.

```bash
curl -sSL instl.sh/openstatushq/cli/macos | bash
```

```bash
curl -sSL instl.sh/openstatushq/cli/linux | bash
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenStatus CLI on macOS

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/cli/getting-started.mdx

Installs the OpenStatus CLI on macOS using Homebrew. This command installs the OpenStatus cask.

```bash
brew install openstatusHQ/cli/openstatus --cask
```

--------------------------------

### Install sqld-beta with Homebrew

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/packages/db/README.md

Installs the sqld-beta package using Homebrew, a package manager for macOS. This command taps the libsql repository and then installs the sqld-beta formula.

```bash
brew tap libsql/sqld
brew install sqld-beta
sqld --help
```

--------------------------------

### Install sqld-beta

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/contributing/setup-env.mdx

Installs the sqld-beta package using Homebrew, a package manager for macOS and Linux. This is a prerequisite for using local Turso SQLite databases.

```bash
brew tap libsql/sqld
brew install sqld-beta
```

--------------------------------

### Vercel Edge Checker Deployment

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/getting-started/introduction.mdx

Instructions for deploying the Vercel Edge Checker version of OpenStatus, a simplified self-hosted option.

```html
<Aside>
    It is not easy to self-host OpenStatus on your own at the moment. We will be working on community version to make it easier in the future.
</Aside>

#### Vercel Edge Checker

You can use our [Vercel Edge Checker](https://github.com/openstatusHQ/vercel-edge-ping) version of OpenStatus. It is a simple version of OpenStatus that can be deployed to Vercel with one click.

<Aside>
More version will be available in the future. 🚧
</Aside>
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenStatus CLI with Homebrew

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/web/src/content/changelog/openstatus-cli.mdx

Installs the OpenStatus CLI by tapping the official Homebrew repository and then installing the package. This is the primary method for getting started with the CLI.

```bash
brew tap openstatusHQ/cli
brew install openstatus
```

--------------------------------

### Astro Project Commands

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/README.md

Lists essential npm commands for managing an Astro project, including dependency installation, starting the development server, building for production, and previewing the build. Also includes commands for interacting with the Astro CLI.

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
npm run build
npm run preview
npm run astro ...
npm run astro -- --help
```

--------------------------------

### Start Development Database with Turso

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/contributing/setup-env.mdx

Starts a local development database using the `turso dev` command, specifying an SQLite database file named `openstatus-dev.db`. This command is used to run the database locally for development and testing purposes.

```bash
turso dev --db-file openstatus-dev.db
```

--------------------------------

### Install and Setup tinybird-cli

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/contributing/setup-env.mdx

Installs the tinybird-cli tool within a Python virtual environment and activates it. This is a prerequisite for connecting to Tinybird datasources.

```bash
cd packages/tinybird
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install tinybird-cli
```

--------------------------------

### Start Local Turso SQLite Database

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/contributing/setup-env.mdx

Starts a local Turso development server with a SQLite database file named 'openstatus.db'. This allows for local database operations without modifying the .env file.

```bash
turso dev --db-file openstatus.db
```

--------------------------------

### Run Local Turso Development Database

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/packages/db/README.md

Starts a local Turso development database. The `--db-file` flag specifies the name of the local database file to be created or used.

```bash
turso dev --db-file openstatus.db
```

--------------------------------

### Setup Tinybird CLI Environment

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/packages/tinybird/README.md

Steps to set up a Python virtual environment, activate it, install the Tinybird CLI, and authenticate with Tinybird.

```bash
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install tinybird-cli
tb auth -i
```

--------------------------------

### Launch Drizzle Studio

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/packages/db/README.md

Starts Drizzle Studio, a GUI tool for inspecting and interacting with your database schema. This command is used to verify that database tables have been created correctly.

```bash
pnpm studio
```

--------------------------------

### Turso Database Configuration in .env

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/contributing/setup-env.mdx

Example of how to configure the Turso database connection details within the .env file, including the database URL and authentication token.

```bash
# TURSO SQLITE
DATABASE_URL=libsql://my-database-example.turso.io
DATABASE_AUTH_TOKEN=ey••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••
```

--------------------------------

### Clone Repository and Install Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/README.md

Steps to clone the OpenStatus repository and install project dependencies using pnpm.

```sh
git clone https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus.git
pnpm install
```

--------------------------------

### Use OpenStatus API Token with CLI Command

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/cli/getting-started.mdx

Provides an API token directly to an OpenStatus CLI command using the `--access-token` option. This is an alternative to setting an environment variable.

```bash
openstatus [COMMAND] --access-token your-api-token
```

--------------------------------

### Create Turso Cloud Database

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/contributing/setup-env.mdx

Creates a new database on the Turso cloud platform. After creation, the command output will provide connection details.

```bash
turso db create
```

--------------------------------

### Fly.io Machine Start Logs

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/web/src/content/posts/monitoring-latency-cf-workers-fly-koyeb-raylway-render.mdx

Example logs from a Fly.io machine startup process, showing the sequence of events from the proxy initiating the start to the machine becoming reachable. It includes timestamps and informational messages about the process.

```bash
2024-02-14T11:24:16.107 proxy[286560ea703108] ams [info] Starting machine

2024-02-14T11:24:16.322 app[286560ea703108] ams [info] [ 0.035736] PCI: Fatal: No config space access function found

2024-02-14T11:24:16.533 app[286560ea703108] ams [info] INFO Starting init (commit: bfa79be)...

2024-02-14T11:24:16.546 app[286560ea703108] ams [info] INFO Preparing to run: `/usr/local/bin/docker-entrypoint.sh bun start` as root

2024-02-14T11:24:16.558 app[286560ea703108] ams [info] INFO [fly api proxy] listening at /.fly/api

2024-02-14T11:24:16.565 app[286560ea703108] ams [info] 2024/02/14 11:24:16 listening on [fdaa:3:2ef:a7b:10c:3c9a:5b4:2]:22 (DNS: [fdaa::3]:53)

2024-02-14T11:24:16.611 app[286560ea703108] ams [info] $ bun src/index.ts

2024-02-14T11:24:16.618 runner[286560ea703108] ams [info] Machine started in 460ms

2024-02-14T11:24:17.621 proxy[286560ea703108] ams [info] machine started in 1.513643778s

2024-02-14T11:24:17.628 proxy[286560ea703108] ams [info] machine became reachable in 7.03669ms
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/workflows/README.md

Installs project dependencies using Bun.

```sh
bun install
```

--------------------------------

### Launch Web Application

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/README.md

Command to start the OpenStatus web application locally.

```sh
pnpm dev:web
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenStatus React Package

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/packages/react/README.md

Instructions for installing the @openstatus/react package using various package managers like npm, pnpm, yarn, and bun.

```bash
npm install @openstatus/react
pnpm add @openstatus/react
yarn add @openstatus/react
bun add @openstatus/react
```

--------------------------------

### Turso Authentication

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/contributing/setup-env.mdx

Logs in the user to their Turso account via the command line interface. This is a necessary step before creating or managing cloud-based databases.

```bash
turso auth login
```

--------------------------------

### Set OpenStatus API Token via Environment Variable

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/cli/getting-started.mdx

Sets the OpenStatus API token as an environment variable. This token is required for authenticating CLI commands.

```bash
export OPENSTATUS_API_TOKEN=your-api-token
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/packages/notifications/discord/README.md

Installs the necessary project dependencies using Bun.

```bash
bun install
```

--------------------------------

### Displaying Monitor Creation Image

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/monitoring/create-monitor.mdx

Renders an image component to visually guide users through the monitor creation process.

```html
<Image
    src={createMonitor}
    alt="Charts with status code and response time"
  />
```

--------------------------------

### Dockerfile for OpenStatus GitHub Action

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/web/src/content/posts/how-we-build-our-github-action.mdx

This Dockerfile sets up an Alpine Linux environment, installs curl, downloads and extracts the OpenStatus CLI, and sets the entrypoint script.

```Dockerfile
FROM alpine:3.21.3

RUN apk --no-cache add curl

WORKDIR /home/openstatus

COPY entrypoint.sh .

RUN  curl -o cli.tar.gz -L  https://github.com/openstatusHQ/cli/releases/latest/download/cli_Linux_x86_64.tar.gz

RUN tar -xf ./cli.tar.gz

ENTRYPOINT ["/home/openstatus/entrypoint.sh"]
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/packages/notifications/webhook/README.md

Installs the necessary project dependencies using the Bun package manager.

```bash
bun install
```

--------------------------------

### Pushing Pipes and Populating Data

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/packages/tinybird/README.md

Example commands for pulling data, pushing aggregate pipes with population, and pushing endpoint pipes.

```bash
tb pull
tb push aggregate_*.pipe --populate
tb push endpoint_*.pipe
```

--------------------------------

### Run Development Server

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/web/README.md

Starts the development server for the OpenStatus project. This command is typically run from the project's root directory.

```bash
pnpm dev
```

--------------------------------

### Install Bun

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/contributing/requirements.mdx

Instructions for installing the Bun JavaScript runtime.

```APIDOC
Bun Installation:
  Visit the official website [https://bun.sh/](https://bun.sh/)
```

--------------------------------

### Fly.toml Configuration for Zero Cold Start

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/web/src/content/posts/monitoring-latency-cf-workers-fly-koyeb-raylway-render.mdx

This snippet shows the configuration for a `fly.toml` file to ensure a minimum number of running machines, which helps achieve zero cold starts and better latency for applications deployed on Fly.io.

```toml
min_machines_running = 1
```

--------------------------------

### Install React Widget Package

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/tools/react.mdx

Installs the OpenStatus React package using npm.

```bash
npm install @openstatus/react
```

--------------------------------

### Installing shadcn Components via CLI

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/web/src/content/posts/new-dashboard-we-are-so-back.mdx

Shows how to install pre-built shadcn components from a remote JSON file using the shadcn CLI. This command fetches and integrates the specified component into your project, streamlining the UI development process.

```bash
pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add https://template.openstatus.dev/r/form-card.json
```

--------------------------------

### Install Turso CLI

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/contributing/requirements.mdx

Provides instructions for installing the Turso CLI for local database usage.

```APIDOC
Turso CLI Installation:
  Follow the instructions in the [Turso CLI](https://docs.turso.tech/reference/turso-cli)
```

--------------------------------

### Run Development Server

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/workflows/README.md

Starts the development server for the Workflows project.

```sh
bun run dev
open <http://localhost:3000>
```

--------------------------------

### Build Docker Image Locally

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/server/README.md

Builds a Docker image locally for the OpenStatus server, tagging it for the Fly registry and specifying the Dockerfile and platform.

```bash
docker build . -t registry.fly.io/openstatus-docker:openstatus-docker-v0  --file  ./apps/server/Dockerfile --platform linux/amd64
```

--------------------------------

### Upstash QStash Configuration

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/contributing/setup-env.mdx

Configuration for QStash environment variables in the .env file, including signing keys and tokens for queue management. These are obtained from the Upstash console.

```bash
# UPSTASH for queue
QSTASH_CURRENT_SIGNING_KEY=sig_•••••••••••••••••••••••••
QSTASH_NEXT_SIGNING_KEY=sig_•••••••••••••••••••••••••
QSTASH_TOKEN=ey••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••
QSTASH_URL=https://qstash.upstash.io/v1/publish/
```

--------------------------------

### Run Database Migration Script

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/packages/db/README.md

Executes the database migration script using pnpm. This command ensures that the database schema is up-to-date with the latest changes.

```bash
pnpm migrate
```

--------------------------------

### Deploy OpenStatus Server with Flyctl

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/server/README.md

Deploys the OpenStatus server application using flyctl, specifying the configuration file and Dockerfile location.

```bash
flyctl deploy --config apps/server/fly.toml --dockerfile  apps/server/Dockerfile
```

--------------------------------

### Generate Dockerfile with Dofigen

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/server/README.md

Commands to update dependent image versions and generate the Dockerfile using Dofigen from the apps/server directory.

```bash
# Update the dependent image versions
dofigen update
# Generate the Dockerfile
dofigen gen
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve Turso Database Information

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/contributing/setup-env.mdx

Displays the connection URL and authentication token for a specified Turso database. This information is crucial for configuring the application's .env file.

```bash
turso db show db-name
turso db tokens create db-name
```

--------------------------------

### Deploy Docker Image to Fly

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/server/README.md

Deploys the specified Docker image to the Fly platform under the 'openstatus-docker' app name.

```bash
flyctl deploy --app openstatus-docker \
  --image registry.fly.io/openstatus-docker:openstatus-docker-v0
```

--------------------------------

### Database Environment Variables

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/packages/db/README.md

Sets up environment variables required for connecting to the local Turso database. These variables should be placed in a `.env` file within the respective project directories.

```env
DATABASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8080
DATABASE_AUTH_TOKEN=any-token
```

--------------------------------

### Resend API Key Configuration

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/contributing/setup-env.mdx

Configuration for the RESEND_API_KEY in the .env file to enable email sending functionality. This requires obtaining an API key from the Resend platform.

```bash
# RESEND for email
RESEND_API_KEY=re_••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••••
```

--------------------------------

### Upstash Redis Configuration

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/contributing/setup-env.mdx

Configuration for Upstash Redis in the .env file, including the REST URL and token. This is used for the waiting list functionality and requires details from the Upstash console.

```bash
# UPSTASH redis for waiting list
UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL="https://•••••••••.upstash.io"
UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN="•••••••••"
```

--------------------------------

### Tinybird API Key Configuration

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/contributing/setup-env.mdx

Configuration for the Tinybird API key in the .env file, used for data ingestion. The API token is generated within the Tinybird platform.

```bash
# TinyBird
TINY_BIRD_API_KEY="•••••••••"
```

--------------------------------

### Project Structure Overview

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/README.md

Illustrates the typical directory and file structure of an Astro project utilizing the Starlight framework. It highlights key directories like `public/` and `src/content/docs/` for assets and documentation files respectively.

```bash
.
├── public/
├── src/
│   ├── assets/
│   ├── content/
│   │   ├── docs/
│   │   └── config.ts
│   └── env.d.ts
├── astro.config.mjs
├── package.json
└── tsconfig.json
```

--------------------------------

### Create Astro Project with Starlight

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/README.md

Command to create a new Astro project using the Starlight template. This is the initial step for setting up a documentation site with Starlight.

```bash
npm create astro@latest -- --template starlight
```

--------------------------------

### OpenTelemetry Setup and Metrics

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/monitoring/opentelemetry.mdx

Instructions for setting up an OTLP endpoint in OpenStatus to export synthetic monitoring data. It also lists the available metrics for HTTP and TCP probes, along with relevant attributes.

```APIDOC
OpenTelemetry Configuration:
  Endpoint: _Select a monitor -> Settings -> OTel_
  Add your OTLP endpoint and include your headers.

Attributes:
  - openstatus.probes: the region of the probes
  - openstatus.target: the target being monitored

Available Metrics (HTTP Probes):
  - openstatus.http.request.duration: Duration of the HTTP request.
  - openstatus.http.dns.duration: Duration of the DNS lookup.
  - openstatus.http.connection.duration: Duration of the TCP connection.
  - openstatus.http.tls.duration: Duration of the TLS handshake.
  - openstatus.http.ttfb.duration: Time to first byte.
  - openstatus.http.transfer.duration: Duration of data transfer.
  - openstatus.error: Counter incremented when an error occurs.
  - openstatus.status: Counter set to 1 for successful requests.

Available Metrics (TCP Probes):
  - openstatus.tcp.request.duration: Duration of the TCP request.
```

--------------------------------

### Video Tutorial Embed

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/monitoring/create-monitor.mdx

Embeds a YouTube video tutorial for creating a monitor.

```html
<ShowcaseYouTube
  entries={[
    {
      href: 'https://www.youtube.com/embed/nYti3DjHoWY?si=RBGFHzoHFmwphRf',
      title: 'Create your first monitor',
    },

  ]}
/>
```

--------------------------------

### Astro Image Component for OpsGenie Setup

Source: https://github.com/openstatushq/openstatus/blob/main/apps/docs/src/content/docs/alerting/providers/opsgenie.mdx

Demonstrates the use of Astro's Image component to display images during the OpsGenie integration setup process. These images visually guide the user through creating an OpsGenie integration and connecting it to OpenStatus.

```javascript
import { Image} from 'astro:assets';
import OpsGenie1 from '../../../../assets/notification/opsgenie/opsgenie-1.png';
import OpsGenie2 from '../../../../assets/notification/opsgenie/opsgenie-2.png';
import OpsGenie3 from '../../../../assets/notification/opsgenie/opsgenie-3.png';
import OpsGenie4 from '../../../../assets/notification/opsgenie/opsgenie-4.png';
import OpsGenie5 from '../../../../assets/notification/opsgenie/opsgenie-5.png';
```
